Give yourself peace of mind by knowing that your cryptocurrencies are safe\",\"og:url\":\"https://www.ledger.com/\",\"og:site_name\":\"Ledger\",\"article:publisher\":\"https://www.facebook.com/Ledger/\",\"article:modified_time\":\"2026-03-24T13:40:33+00:00\",\"og:image\":\"https://ledger-wp-website-s3-prd.ledger.com/uploads/2025/10/og_home.webp\",\"og:image:width\":\"1200\",\"og:image:height\":\"640\",\"og:image:type\":\"image/webp\",\"twitter:card\":\"summary_large_image\",\"twitter:site\":\"@ledger\"}","microdata":"[{\"dimensions\":{\"h\":1071,\"w\":800},\"properties\":{},\"subscopes\":[{\"dimensions\":{\"h\":0,\"w\":0},\"properties\":{\"name\":\"What is a crypto wallet?\"},\"subscopes\":[{\"dimensions\":{\"h\":0,\"w\":0},\"properties\":{\"text\":\"Thinking about buying crypto or NFTs? You’ll need a crypto wallet.\\nWhen you create a wallet, two keys are generated: a private one and a public one. The wallet stores your keys and enables you to sign transactions, generate new addresses, initiate transfers, track portfolio balances, manage your crypto, and interact with dApps.\\nCrypto wallets come in many forms, from hardware wallets, like Ledger’s, to mobile apps that you can download on your phone or tablet.\"},\"subscopes\":[],\"type\":\"https://schema.org/Answer\"}],\"type\":\"https://schema.org/Question\"},{\"dimensions\":{\"h\":0,\"w\":0},\"properties\":{\"name\":\"How do crypto wallets work?\"},\"subscopes\":[{\"dimensions\":{\"h\":0,\"w\":0},\"properties\":{\"text\":\"When you buy crypto like Bitcoin and Ethereum, you’re issued two keys: one is public and the other is private.\\nThe public key can be compared to a bank account number that you can share with third parties to receive crypto without worrying that your assets will be compromised.\\nThe private key signs transactions and allows you to send and receive crypto. It’s crucial to keep your private keys secure and secret. If anyone has access to them, they will also have access to any crypto assets associat\"},\"subscopes\":[],\"type\":\"https://schema.org/Answer\"}],\"type\":\"https://schema.org/Question\"},{\"dimensions\":{\"h\":0,\"w\":0},\"properties\":{\"name\":\"What are the different types of crypto wallets?\"},\"subscopes\":[{\"dimensions\":{\"h\":0,\"w\":0},\"properties\":{\"text\":\"There are different types of crypto wallets, each with its own benefits and drawbacks.\\nHot wallets are connected to the internet and usually convenient to use, however, they are also vulnerable to online attacks. Examples include web-based, mobile, and desktop wallets. Cold wallets keep your private keys offline and out of reach of online threats. Examples include paper and hardware wallets.\\nWallets can also be categorized as custodial or non-custodial, depending on who holds the private keys. S\"},\"subscopes\":[],\"type\":\"https://schema.org/Answer\"}],\"type\":\"https://schema.org/Question\"},{\"dimensions\":{\"h\":0,\"w\":0},\"properties\":{\"name\":\"Why do I need a hardware wallet?\"},\"subscopes\":[{\"dimensions\":{\"h\":0,\"w\":0},\"properties\":{\"text\":\"Hot wallets store private keys on systems connected to the internet, which makes them susceptible to online attacks. Keeping your crypto on an exchange also means you have no true ownership or control over it. If the exchange files for bankruptcy or pauses withdrawals, you lose access to your funds.\\nHardware wallets store your private keys offline, giving you full control and enhanced security. Even if you misplace or lose your hardware wallet, you can get a new one and use your Secret Recovery \"},\"subscopes\":[],\"type\":\"https://schema.org/Answer\"}],\"type\":\"https://schema.org/Question\"},{\"dimensions\":{\"h\":0,\"w\":0},\"properties\":{\"name\":\"How to get a crypto wallet?\"},\"subscopes\":[{\"dimensions\":{\"h\":0,\"w\":0},\"properties\":{\"text\":\"Ready to get started? Here are the steps for getting your crypto wallet:\\n\\nGet a Ledger hardware wallet. It stores your private keys in a secure, offline environment giving you peace of mind and complete control over your assets. All Ledger crypto wallets are powered by an industry-leading Secure Element chip, together with Ledger’s proprietary OS that protects your crypto & NFTs from sophisticated hacks.\\nPair your Ledger crypto wallet with the Ledger Wallet app to easily manage your crypto, keep\"},\"subscopes\":[],\"type\":\"https://schema.org/Answer\"}],\"type\":\"https://schema.org/Question\"},{\"dimensions\":{\"h\":327,\"w\":623},\"properties\":{\"name\":\"What is a crypto wallet?\"},\"subscopes\":[{\"dimensions\":{\"h\":239,\"w\":623},\"properties\":{\"text\":\"Thinking about buying crypto or NFTs? You’ll need a crypto wallet.\\n\\nWhen you create a wallet, two keys are generated: a private one and a public one. The wallet stores your keys and enables you to sign transactions, generate new addresses, initiate transfers, track portfolio balances, manage your crypto, and interact with dApps.\\n\\nCrypto wallets come in many forms, from hardware wallets, like Ledger’s, to mobile apps that you can download on your phone or tablet.\"},\"subscopes\":[],\"type\":\"https://schema.org/Answer\"}],\"type\":\"https://schema.org/Question\"},{\"dimensions\":{\"h\":68,\"w\":623},\"properties\":{\"name\":\"How do crypto wallets work?\"},\"subscopes\":[{\"dimensions\":{\"h\":0,\"w\":623},\"properties\":{\"text\":\"When you buy crypto like Bitcoin and Ethereum, you’re issued two keys: one is public and the other is private.\\n\\nThe public key can be compared to a bank account number that you can share with third parties to receive crypto without worrying that your assets will be compromised.\\n\\nThe private key signs transactions and allows you to send and receive crypto. It’s crucial to keep your private keys secure and secret. If anyone has access to them, they will also have access to any crypto assets associ\"},\"subscopes\":[],\"type\":\"https://schema.org/Answer\"}],\"type\":\"https://schema.org/Question\"},{\"dimensions\":{\"h\":68,\"w\":623},\"properties\":{\"name\":\"What are the different types of crypto wallets?\"},\"subscopes\":[{\"dimensions\":{\"h\":0,\"w\":623},\"properties\":{\"text\":\"There are different types of crypto wallets, each with its own benefits and drawbacks.\\nHot wallets are connected to the internet and usually convenient to use, however, they are also vulnerable to online attacks. Examples include web-based, mobile, and desktop wallets. Cold wallets keep your private keys offline and out of reach of online threats. Examples include paper and hardware wallets.\\n\\nWallets can also be categorized as custodial or non-custodial, depending on who holds the private keys. \"},\"subscopes\":[],\"type\":\"https://schema.org/Answer\"}],\"type\":\"https://schema.org/Question\"},{\"dimensions\":{\"h\":68,\"w\":623},\"properties\":{\"name\":\"Why do I need a hardware wallet?\"},\"subscopes\":[{\"dimensions\":{\"h\":0,\"w\":623},\"properties\":{\"text\":\"Hot wallets store private keys on systems connected to the internet, which makes them susceptible to online attacks. Keeping your crypto on an exchange also means you have no true ownership or control over it. If the exchange files for bankruptcy or pauses withdrawals, you lose access to your funds.\\n\\nHardware wallets store your private keys offline, giving you full control and enhanced security. Even if you misplace or lose your hardware wallet, you can get a new one and use your Secret Recovery\"},\"subscopes\":[],\"type\":\"https://schema.org/Answer\"}],\"type\":\"https://schema.org/Question\"},{\"dimensions\":{\"h\":68,\"w\":623},\"properties\":{\"name\":\"How to get a crypto wallet?\"},\"subscopes\":[{\"dimensions\":{\"h\":0,\"w\":623},\"properties\":{\"text\":\"Ready to get started? Here are the steps for getting your crypto wallet:\\n\\nGet a Ledger hardware wallet. It stores your private keys in a secure, offline environment giving you peace of mind and complete control over your assets. All Ledger crypto wallets are powered by an industry-leading Secure Element chip, together with Ledger’s proprietary OS that protects your crypto & NFTs from sophisticated hacks.\\nPair your Ledger crypto wallet with the Ledger Wallet app to easily manage your crypto, keep\"},\"subscope
